Tests arithmetic operations with Infinity in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of arithmetic operations involving the `Infinity` value in JavaScript. It verifies that adding `Infinity` to `Infinity` results in `Infinity` and that adding `-Infinity` to `-Infinity` results in `-Infinity`. This test ensures that the JavaScript engine correctly handles these specific cases of arithmetic operations with infinite values. The code is narrow in functionality, focusing solely on validating these two arithmetic expressions.
